summary: E-commerce website for 'Prodal Tex' selling premium Polyester Knitted dyed fabric and clothing (t-shirts, lowers, coats). The site has two product sections: 'Clothing' and 'Fabrics' — both currently empty with no products; products will be added later. The Clothing sub-category columns (T-Shirts, Lowers, Coats) are being removed from the homepage/products page.
architectural_notes: Niche: premium Polyester Knitted dyed fabric sales
Brand name displayed as 'PRODAL TEX' (all caps)
Admin dashboard (/admin) must be protected and only accessible to authorized admin users
Admin access is now password-protected with password 'luckygoyal0001' — no Internet Identity or Google login
Site now has two product categories: 'Clothing' (t-shirts, lowers, coats) and 'Fabrics' — clothing items to be populated later
Both 'Clothing' and 'Fabrics' sections currently have no products — products will be added later by the owner
known_issues: 'Request a Quote' button/section not visible due to white-on-white color contrast issue
Homepage hero/profile photo is missing or not displaying
Admin dashboard shows 'refresh the page' message instead of displaying inquiries

existing_features.title: Create a visually striking homepage for 'Prodal Tex' that includes a hero section with brand headline, a brief brand introduction emphasizing premium quality Polyester Knitted dyed fabric, a featured products/collections section, key selling points (e.g., quality, variety of colors, bulk availability), and a call-to-action directing visitors to browse products or contact for orders.

Build a product listing page displaying all available fabric products in a responsive grid. Each product card should show the fabric image, name, color/shade, GSM, price indication, and a 'View Details' button. Include a color/shade filter to narrow down results.

Build a product detail page that shows the full information for a selected fabric product: large image, full description, fabric type, all color/shade options, GSM, width, minimum order quantity, price, and an 'Inquire Now' button that links to the contact/inquiry form.

Create a Contact/Inquiry page with a form for wholesale and retail customers to request pricing or place inquiries. Fields: customer name, email, phone, company name (optional), product of interest (dropdown populated from product list), quantity required, and a message/notes field. On submission, save the inquiry to the backend and display a success confirmation message.

Implement a site-wide navigation header showing the 'Prodal Tex' logo/brand name and links to: Home, Products, and Contact. Add a footer with brand name, tagline, and contact information placeholder. Navigation must be responsive with a mobile hamburger menu.

Apply a sophisticated, textile-industry-inspired visual theme across the entire site: deep charcoal and warm off-white as primary tones, with a rich teal or deep forest-green accent color. Use elegant serif or refined sans-serif typography for headings, generous whitespace, and subtle fabric-texture-inspired visual motifs. The design should evoke premium quality and professionalism consistent with a high-end fabric brand.

Render the generated fabric texture hero banner image (fabric-hero-banner.dim_1400x600.png) on the homepage hero section and the product card placeholder images (fabric-swatch-placeholder.dim_600x600.png) for products without assigned images, sourced from frontend/public/assets/generated.

Update every user-facing display of the brand name across the entire site to render as 'PRODAL TEX' (all caps). This includes the Header logo/brand name, Footer brand name, page titles, hero section headings, meta title in index.html, and any other location where 'Prodal Tex' appears as displayed text.

Update all contact information displayed across the site to use the real business details: phone number 9978625857, email Lakshaypgoyal@gmail.com, and address 'Shop No. UG 18/A to 27/A, Sangini Trade Center, Sarolikumbhariya Road, Kubharia Gaon, Sayam Sangini, Surat-395010'. This must be reflected in the Footer component, the Contact page, and any other location where placeholder contact information currently appears.

Fix the 'Request a Quote' / 'Get a Quote' button and any associated section or CTA element that is currently invisible due to white text or white background rendering on a white surface. Ensure the button has sufficient color contrast by applying the brand's teal/forest-green accent color as the background with white or off-white text, or vice versa, so it is clearly visible against its surrounding background across all pages and contexts where it appears (Header, Hero section, any CTA banners).
Add a React Query hook in useQueries.ts to fetch all inquiries from the backend using the new query function added in REQ-13.

Create an Admin Dashboard page at the route '/admin' that displays all submitted customer inquiries in a styled table. Columns must include: customer name, email, phone number, company name, product of interest, quantity required, message, and submission date/time. The page should match the site's existing brand theme (deep charcoal, off-white, teal accent). Include a loading state and an empty state message if no inquiries have been submitted yet.
Add an 'Admin' link in the site header's navigation (desktop and mobile) pointing to '/admin', so the site owner can easily access the dashboard from any page.
Register the '/admin' route in App.tsx so the Admin Dashboard page is accessible via client-side routing.

Add Internet Identity authentication-based access control to the '/admin' route. If the user is not logged in (not authenticated via Internet Identity), redirect them away from '/admin' to the homepage or show an 'Access Denied' / login prompt screen. The Admin Dashboard page content (inquiry table) must only be rendered for authenticated users.

Remove or hide the 'Admin' navigation link in the site header (desktop and mobile) for unauthenticated users. The Admin link should only be visible in the header when the user is authenticated via Internet Identity.

Add a login/logout button in the site header that allows the admin to sign in and out via Internet Identity. When unauthenticated, show a 'Login' button. When authenticated, show a 'Logout' button (and optionally the principal/identity indicator). Use the existing useInternetIdentity hook for auth state and actions.

On the Admin Dashboard page (`AdminDashboardPage.tsx`), after the user authenticates via Internet Identity, call the backend `isAuthorized` check (or handle the error response from `getInquiries`). If the authenticated user's principal is not authorized, display an 'Access Denied' message explaining that this dashboard is restricted to the site owner, and do not render the inquiry table. Only the specific authorized principal(s) associated with lakshaypgoyal@gmail.com or phone number 9978625857 should see the inquiry data.

Remove all Internet Identity authentication from the admin flow. Replace it with a simple client-side password prompt on the Admin Dashboard page. The user must enter the password 'luckygoyal0001' to access the inquiries table. If the password is incorrect, display an error message. If correct, render the inquiries table. No other login method (Internet Identity, Google, or any third-party) should remain anywhere in the application.

Remove the login/logout button and any Internet Identity-related UI controls from the site Header component (both desktop and mobile layouts). The header should no longer reference or use the useInternetIdentity hook for rendering login/logout controls.

Ensure the homepage hero/profile section displays a visible photo. The hero banner image (fabric-hero-banner.dim_1400x600.png) from frontend/public/assets/generated must be rendered and clearly visible in the hero section of HomePage.tsx. Diagnose and fix any CSS, path, or rendering issue that causes the image to not appear.

Add a 'Clothing' category section on the homepage and/or products page. This section should display a heading 'Clothing', a brief description (e.g., 'Premium ready-made garments'), and placeholder cards for the sub-categories: T-Shirts, Lowers, and Coats. Since no clothing products exist in the backend yet, display visually styled placeholder/coming-soon cards for each sub-category. The section should match the site's existing brand theme (deep charcoal, off-white, teal accent).

Add a 'Fabrics' category section on the homepage and/or products page that groups and displays the existing fabric products. This section should have a clear 'Fabrics' heading and render the existing fabric product cards (or a subset as featured items) beneath it. This clearly separates fabric products from the new Clothing section.

Update the 'Fabrics' category section on the homepage (HomePage.tsx) and the Products page (ProductsPage.tsx) to display a clear empty state when no fabric products exist. Instead of showing sample/auto-initialized products or a blank space, render a styled 'No products yet — check back soon!' (or similar) placeholder message within the Fabrics section. Remove or disable any auto-initialization logic that pre-populates sample fabric products on first load, so the section truly reflects zero products.

Update the 'Clothing' category section on the homepage (HomePage.tsx) to display a clear empty state for each sub-category (T-Shirts, Lowers, Coats) indicating that no products are available yet. Replace any 'Coming Soon' placeholder cards with a unified empty-state message or minimal placeholder that clearly communicates products will be added later. The section heading 'Clothing' and its brief description must remain visible.

Disable or remove the backend auto-initialization call (initializeSampleProducts or equivalent) that is triggered on the frontend (e.g., in HomePage.tsx or useQueries.ts) when no products are found. The site should not auto-populate the backend with sample fabric products; the product list should remain empty until products are manually added.

Remove the T-Shirts, Lowers, and Coats sub-category columns/cards from the Clothing section on the homepage (HomePage.tsx). The Clothing section heading ('Clothing') and its brief description must remain visible, but all three sub-category placeholder entries must be deleted from the rendered output.

Diagnose and fix the data fetching issue in AdminDashboardPage.tsx that causes the inquiries table to display a 'refresh the page' message instead of rendering submitted inquiries. Investigate the useGetAllInquiries React Query hook in useQueries.ts and the corresponding backend getInquiries function in backend/main.mo. Ensure the query correctly calls the backend actor, handles the response (including Motoko Result/variant unwrapping), and surfaces real inquiry data in the table. Eliminate any condition or error path that triggers the 'refresh the page' fallback message when inquiries are present or the fetch is in progress.

new_feature_requests.summary: Homepage showcasing premium Polyester Knitted dyed fabric brand and products
Product listing and detail pages for fabric collections
Contact/inquiry form for wholesale or retail customers

Implement a backend data model and API for fabric products, each with fields: name, description, fabric type (default: 'Polyester Knitted Dyed'), color/shade options, weight (GSM), available width, minimum order quantity, price per meter/kg, and an image filename. Pre-populate with at least 6 sample fabric products across different colors and styles.

Implement a backend model and API to store customer inquiries submitted via the contact form. Each inquiry should capture: customer name, email, phone number, company name (optional), product of interest, quantity required, and message.
Update brand/site name display to 'PRODAL TEX' (all caps) throughout the site
Update site contact details: phone 9978625857, email Lakshaypgoyal@gmail.com, address Shop No. UG 18/A to 27/A, Sangini Trade Center, Sarolikumbhariya Road, Kubharia Gaon, Sayam Sangini, Surat-395010
Build an admin dashboard to view and manage all submitted quote/inquiry requests, showing customer name, email, phone, product interest, quantity, and message

Add a backend query function to retrieve all submitted CustomerInquiry records, returning the full list of inquiries including customer name, email, phone number, company name, product of interest, quantity required, message, and submission timestamp.

Replace Internet Identity login with a simple password-based admin login. The password should be 'luckygoyal0001'. Remove any Google/Internet Identity login. Only users with the correct password can access the inquiries/admin dashboard.

Update the backend access control so that the getInquiries function is publicly callable without requiring a specific authorized principal. Since authentication is now handled client-side via password, the backend should allow any caller to fetch inquiries (or keep existing logic but not block based on Internet Identity principal).
Ensure the homepage hero/profile section displays a photo (keep existing photo visible on the front page)
Add two product category sections on the homepage/products page: one for 'Clothing' (t-shirts, lowers, coats to be added later) and one for 'Fabrics'
Remove all pre-added/sample fabric products from the backend/frontend. The fabrics section should be completely empty until the owner manually specifies which products to add.

Delete all existing fabric product records stored in the backend. The backend's product list must be completely empty after this operation. No fabric products should be returned by any query (e.g., getProducts) until the owner manually adds them.

Ensure the Fabrics section on the homepage (HomePage.tsx) and the Products page (ProductsPage.tsx) correctly reflects the now-empty backend by showing the empty-state UI ('No products yet — check back soon!' or equivalent) immediately, with no lingering cached or hardcoded product data displayed.

Remove the T-Shirts, Lowers, and Coats sub-category columns/cards from the Clothing section on the homepage and products page. The Clothing section heading and description may remain, but the sub-category placeholders should be removed.
Fix admin dashboard: inquiries are not displaying and instead showing a 'refresh the page' message. Investigate and resolve the data fetching/loading issue so submitted inquiries appear correctly.
